name: singleton_unique_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_1_singleton_unique_symmetric(s: Set<nat>, a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        s.finite(),
        s.len() == 1,
        s.contains(a),
        s.contains(b),
    ensures
        b == a,
{
    singleton_set_unique_elt(s, a, b);
}
reason: Symmetry of the uniqueness result — trivially follows from `a == b`, testing the basic consequence

name: singleton_element_is_choose
reason: Any element in a singleton equals `choose()` — a correct consequence but shows the lemma determines `choose()` for singletons

name: singleton_insert_same
reason: A concrete singleton `{a}` contains only `a` — correct but tests the lemma on a constructed singleton

name: remove_from_singleton_empty
reason: Removing the sole element from a singleton yields empty — tests the boundary interaction between `remove` and `len`

name: singleton_no_second_element
reason: A singleton set cannot contain a distinct element — correct contrapositive of uniqueness, but confirms the lemma's full power
